channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian and US markets decline as oil prices spike amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Australian shares are expected to open lower, mirroring losses on Wall Street as oil prices surged past US$83 a barrel. The rise in oil has heightened inflation concerns globally, with geopolitical te...

item.title: Karnataka Cyber Command to Launch Special Wing for Monitoring Child Abuse Material Online
item.description: In a significant move to strengthen online child protection, the Karnataka Cyber Command Unit (CCU) is planning to establish a dedicated wing focused on monitoring and investigating cases involving Ch...

item.title: Digitisation alone cannot solve South Africa's university overcrowding
item.description: Mlamuli Hlatshwayo discusses the limitations of digitising higher education as a solution to the growing demand for university access in South Africa. Despite the increasing uptake of digital learning...

item.title: Diplomatic Deal Enables Limited Evacuation of Australians from the Gulf Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: A delicate diplomatic agreement has been reached to allow some Australians stranded in the Gulf region to return home amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. More than 100,000 Australians, includ...

item.title: Duterte Remains in ICC Custody as Judges Deliberate on Crimes Against Humanity Case
item.description: Former President Rodrigo Duterte is still in detention at the International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague, The Netherlands, as the court's judges deliberate whether there is enough evidence to pro...

item.title: Young Africans Secure 3-0 Victory Over Singida Black Stars to Extend Unbeaten League Run
item.description: Defending champions Young Africans (Yanga) continued their dominant form in the Tanzania Mainland Premier League with a convincing 3-0 win against Singida Black Stars at Airtel Stadium on March 5, 202...

item.title: CV Ananda Bose resigns as West Bengal Governor before Assembly polls; RN Ravi likely successor
item.description: West Bengal Governor CV Ananda Bose has resigned from his post, submitting his resignation to President Droupadi Murmu. Bose, who took office in November 2022, reportedly told PTI that he felt he had ...

item.title: Understanding Why a Zoo-Housed Japanese Macaque Infant Was Abandoned by His Mother
item.description: Little Punch, a seven-month-old Japanese macaque at the Ichikawa City Zoo in Japan, became the focus of public attention after his mother abandoned him in the first days of his life. Raised by zoo kee...

item.title: Pakistan urges Afghan Taliban to take concrete actions for ceasefire amid border tensions
item.description: A senior Pakistani security official has stated that the Afghan Taliban fully understand Pakistan’s security concerns and that the onus is on them to take verifiable actions if they want a ceasefire o...

item.title: Solar Energy in Rural Zimbabwe: Women Still Bear the Burden of Unpaid Work
item.description: For rural Zingondi, Zimbabwe, solar energy dey enter homes, but e never change how women dey handle unpaid work for house. Even with small solar lanterns and panels to charge phones, women still dey c...

item.title: The Increasing Focus on Male Beauty Standards and Cosmetic Procedures
item.description: The scrutiny of men’s faces has intensified dramatically in recent years, with more men seeking cosmetic procedures and grooming interventions than ever before. High-profile figures such as Donald Tru...

item.title: John Senona elected as Ekurhuleni council speaker
item.description: The Ekurhuleni council has officially elected John Senona of the African Transformation Movement (ATM) as its new council speaker, receiving a total of 94 votes from councillors. His opponent, Nkulule...

item.title: Parliament reimburses investigator R80,000 for initial two-day testimony before ad hoc committee
item.description: Private investigator Paul O’Sullivan revealed that Parliament paid him R80,000 for his first two-day appearance before an ad hoc committee investigating alleged corruption and political interference i...

item.title: Kenya Reports Increase in Influenza Cases, Ministry of Health Advises Preventive Measures
item.description: The Ministry of Health has raised alarm over a noticeable rise in influenza cases across multiple regions in Kenya. According to Dr Patrick Amoth, Health Director General, surveillance data from the K...

item.title: Performance of Dollar, Government Bonds and Gold as Safe Havens Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has once again pushed investors to look for safe places to park their money, but traditional safe havens are not behaving as expected this time. The US dollar h...

item.title: Paul O’Sullivan responds to ActionSA corruption claims, confirms past donations
item.description: Forensic investigator Paul O’Sullivan has publicly denied allegations of corruption linked to ActionSA, while acknowledging that he made donations to the party when it was first established. Speaking ...

item.title: Nova Scotia disability advocates criticise proposed budget cuts to support programs and caregiver benefits
item.description: Parents and advocates in Nova Scotia are expressing deep concern and feelings of betrayal over the Progressive Conservative government's proposed budget cuts, which disproportionately affect people wi...

item.title: Akor Adams Describes Ademola Lookman as One of the Quietest in Super Eagles Camp
item.description: Super Eagles striker Akor Adams recently shared insights about his teammate Ademola Lookman during an interview with Sporty TV. He pointed out that Lookman, along with Semi Ajayi, is among the quietes...

item.title: Albanese Government Backs US-Israeli Strikes on Iran Amid Political and Social Challenges
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese have expressed support for the recent US-Israeli strikes on Iran, citing concerns about nuclear threats and terrorism...

item.title: FG approves promotions for 70,000 paramilitary officers and strengthens immigration operations
item.description: The Federal Government (FG) don announce say over 70,000 paramilitary officers don get promotion for the past three years. Dr. Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, the Minister of Interior, yan this one for Abuja duri...
